task.objective: Implement the first deterministic Proofrail kernel vertical slice using synthetic in-memory domain inputs and only the authorized contracts and kernel package layers.

forbidden: LLM judgment in the authoritative path
probabilistic confidence in the authoritative path

requirements: Materialize governance/tasks/KERNEL-VS-001.json as the first task artifact and faithfully represent the externally supplied Machine Task Contract.
Keep authority.mayChangeAuthority false and authority.mayChangeProductSemantics false.
Implement only two production package layers: packages/contracts and packages/kernel.
Do not create a third production package or application layer.
Preserve the dependency direction: kernel may depend on contracts, and contracts must not import kernel.
Use pnpm workspace structure with pnpm-workspace.yaml matching only packages/*.
Use the package identities @proofrail/contracts and @proofrail/kernel.
Make both packages private Phase 1 workspace packages.
Allow @proofrail/kernel to depend on @proofrail/contracts through a pnpm workspace dependency.
Ensure @proofrail/contracts has no runtime dependency on kernel or any later layer.
Do not add a production runtime dependency from the public package registry.
Add only the authorized external development dependencies: typescript 5.8.3 and @types/node compatible with Node 24.
Keep Ajv as Foundation governance tooling only; product contracts or kernel source must not import Ajv.
Use executable ESM JavaScript for Phase 1 production source.
Use TypeScript declaration files and/or JSDoc type imports so contracts and kernel source are checked under strict TypeScript checkJs.

Create a root tsconfig.json for strict Phase 1 static checking with allowJs, checkJs, noEmit, strict, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, module NodeNext, and moduleResolution NodeNext.
Do not create a transpilation, build-output, dist, build, coverage, generated runtime, or compiled test directory requirement for pnpm verify.
Use Node's built-in node:test runner and do not add Vitest, Jest, Mocha, tsx, Babel, swc, esbuild, or another test/transpilation framework.
Expose root scripts typecheck:phase1, test:kernel, and verify.
Ensure pnpm verify preserves existing Foundation governance checks and additionally runs Phase 1 static checking and kernel tests while remaining non-mutating.
Update the root package version from the Foundation-only version to 0.1.0-phase1.
Do not claim the repository is a complete product runtime.

Define and expose the minimum strict domain shapes needed for Claim, Evidence Contract, Evidence Requirement, Observation, Evidence, Rule, Verdict, Evidence Lineage, Evidence Bundle, KernelEvaluationInput, and Evidence Contract selection provenance.
Keep contracts structural and do not implement evaluation behavior in contracts.
Do not create parallel synonyms for canonical domain concepts.
Define only the Verdict values ADMISSIBLE, REVISION_REQUIRED, REJECTED, and BLOCKED; do not define PASS, FAIL, APPROVED, DENIED, or UNKNOWN as Verdict values.
Keep Claim non-evidentiary and structurally distinguishable from Observation and Evidence.
Make kernel input a synthetic in-memory domain input supplied directly to the kernel boundary.

Do not implement acquisition of Trusted Configuration, Policy selection, external trust establishment, repository inspection, verification execution, adapters, delivery surfaces, model providers, or inference.
Model Evidence Contract selection provenance with only TRUSTED_CONFIGURATION and DETERMINISTIC_POLICY_SELECTION as allowed sources.
Reject natural-language task requests, Machine Task Contracts, MODEL_OUTPUT, and INFERENCE_PROPOSAL as Evidence Contract selection sources.
Document that the Phase 1 synthetic kernel boundary models already-authorized selection provenance and does not prove Trusted Configuration or deterministic Policy selection outside the kernel.
Implement one narrow deterministic Evidence satisfaction rule equivalent to OBSERVATION_FACT_EQUALS.
Model Evidence Requirements with requirement identity, target scope identity, required observer identity, required observer version, fact key, and expected JSON primitive value.

Model Observations with observation identity, observer identity, observer version, target scope identity, normalized fact key, normalized JSON primitive fact value, source input identity, deterministic ordering key, and limitations.
Satisfy an Evidence Requirement only when scope identity, observer identity, observer version, fact key, expected primitive value, and absence of limitations match deterministically.
Do not use fuzzy matching, natural-language interpretation, model confidence, or coercive equality.
Reject NaN and Infinity as JSON primitive values and keep the number 1 distinct from the string "1".
Use Observations only as Evidence satisfaction inputs; do not ingest Verification Receipts in this task.
Create Evidence for satisfied requirements tied to the exact Evidence Requirement, accepted Observation identities, target scope, evaluation identity, and source lineage.
Ensure a Claim alone or matching Claim statement never creates Evidence.
Implement only minimum deterministic Rule behavior with narrow EVIDENCE_PRESENT or EVIDENCE_ABSENT predicates against an Evidence Requirement identity.
Implement only deterministic denial as the required Rule effect.
A Rule denial must emit the Rule's declared stable reason code, create a REJECTED candidate Verdict, and preserve the Rule identity in Evidence Lineage.
Model Rule authority provenance with only TRUSTED_CONFIGURATION and POLICY as allowed sources.
Require POLICY authority references to include Policy identity and version and TRUSTED_CONFIGURATION authority references to include configuration identity and version.
Reject MACHINE_TASK_CONTRACT, MODEL_OUTPUT, and INFERENCE_PROPOSAL as Rule authority provenance.
Do not implement Policy evaluation or pretend Rule authority references prove external trust.
Perform deterministic runtime input validation at the public kernel evaluation boundary; type declarations alone are insufficient.
Reject structurally unknown authoritative inputs, unexpected authority-shaped fields, functions, symbols, bigint, Date, Map, Set, undefined, cyclic input, NaN, and Infinity.

Validate top-level shape, plain objects, exact known authoritative fields, JSON-compatible primitive constraints, finite numbers only, non-empty stable identities, duplicate identities, references, scope consistency, provenance, and reason-code shape.
Reject unexpected authority-shaped fields modelConfidence, inferenceProposal, and proposedContent before authoritative evaluation.
Do not mutate caller input, do not freeze caller-owned objects, and structurally disconnect normalized input from caller objects.
Create a typed KernelBoundaryError with deterministic issue category and relative input path; do not treat it as a Proofrail Verdict or Evidence Bundle reason code.

Normalize deterministically by cloning accepted input, sorting Evidence Requirements, Observations, and Rules, normalizing object key order for digest serialization, preserving modeled semantic array order, and rejecting duplicate identities before lossy normalization.
Implement canonical JSON serialization that recursively sorts object keys, preserves normalized array order, accepts only JSON-compatible values, and produces stable bytes.

Do not use Date.now, new Date, Math.random, crypto.randomUUID, random UUID packages, process uptime, host identity, absolute repository paths, or environment-variable values as authoritative evaluation inputs or identity sources.
Use deterministic cryptographic hashing, preferably SHA-256, only from canonical deterministic content.
Evaluate every Evidence Requirement deterministically and produce one Evidence record per satisfied requirement with deterministic accepted Observation identity ordering and Evidence Lineage records.
Classify each unsatisfied applicable Evidence Requirement as a REVISION_REQUIRED candidate using KERNEL_EVIDENCE_REQUIREMENT_MISSING while preserving requirement identity in Evidence Lineage.
Evaluate Rules after Evidence satisfaction on normalized inputs and create Rule Evidence Lineage.
Reject Rule reason codes beginning with HARN_ and do not create a general product reason-code registry.
Implement pure deterministic Verdict reduction preserving BLOCKED > REJECTED > REVISION_REQUIRED > ADMISSIBLE, every applicable reason code, and all candidate lineage references.
Produce ADMISSIBLE only when every applicable Evidence Requirement is satisfied, no deterministic Rule denial triggers, and no higher-precedence classified condition exists.
Do not add an unauthorized execution or capability model solely to produce BLOCKED end-to-end; test BLOCKED through the pure reducer.

Implement explicit Evidence Lineage covering Claim, selected Evidence Contract, Evidence Contract selection provenance, Evidence Requirement, accepted Observation, produced Evidence, evaluated Rule, Verdict candidate classification, and Verdict reduction.
Implement a Phase 1 vertical-slice Evidence Bundle without claiming the complete final bundle protocol exists.

Preserve bundle identity, schema version, kernel engine version, evaluation identity, Claim, Evidence Contract, Evidence Contract selection provenance, Evidence Requirements, Observations, produced Evidence, Rules, Evidence Lineage, winning Verdict, retained reason codes, and deterministic Verdict reduction record.
Represent Verification Receipts as empty or omitted only if contracts state this Phase 1 slice does not ingest Verification Receipts.

Do not create fake Verification Receipts, repository snapshot data, adapter identities, Adapter Capability data, environment identity, Policy digest data without a modeled Policy, or fabricated future bundle fields.
Derive bundle identity deterministically from canonical finalized bundle content excluding the bundle identity field itself.
Do not include timestamps, random identifiers, machine paths, host identity, or network-derived values in bundles.
Deeply freeze finalized Evidence Bundles including nested arrays and records, without freezing or mutating caller-owned input.

Expose one clear primary vertical-slice entry point, preferably evaluateKernel or evaluate, that validates input, normalizes it, evaluates requirements, creates Evidence, evaluates Rules, classifies candidate Verdicts, reduces Verdict, assembles Evidence Lineage, finalizes the bundle, and returns it.
Do not expose mark_pass, force_admissible, ignore_missing_evidence, fabricate_receipt, trust_my_claim, semantic equivalents, or a function accepting a desired Verdict as authoritative input.
Do not accept model confidence or Builder assertions that requirements are satisfied.

Use a maintainable module decomposition and do not create a giant single-file kernel, TODO placeholder files, abstract classes, plugin registries, dependency injection containers, event buses, framework scaffolding, repository adapter interfaces, verification provider interfaces, or model provider interfaces.
Add node:test and node:assert/strict tests using synthetic in-memory inputs only.
Tests must not inspect the repository, read files, execute child processes, execute target code, call network services, or read environment variables as authoritative input.

Cover the required test cases for satisfied requirement, Claim is not Evidence, missing Evidence Requirement, observer mismatch, observer version mismatch, scope mismatch, primitive type difference, limited Observation, deterministic Rule denial, Verdict precedence, lower-precedence reason retention, lineage coverage, deterministic repeat evaluation, input order normalization, caller input immutability, caller mutation isolation, deep bundle immutability, model confidence rejection, Inference Proposal rejection, invalid Evidence Contract selection authority, invalid Rule authority, HARN_ reason-code rejection, invalid JSON values, duplicate identities, invalid references, and no random or time input.

Create docs/engineering/kernel-vertical-slice.md as an implementation record, not product authority, documenting package boundary, synthetic boundary, provenance limitations, OBSERVATION_FACT_EQUALS, Rule predicates, normalization, canonical serialization, identity strategy, Verdict reduction, Evidence Lineage, immutability, non-goals, and future gaps.

Update README honestly so it states the repository contains an initial deterministic synthetic-input kernel vertical slice and still lacks repository inspection, verification execution, adapters, delivery surfaces, and a complete product runtime.

Update docs/plans/active/phase-1-deterministic-kernel-vertical-slice.md to record KERNEL-VS-001 Builder implementation exists on the task branch and is pending independent review without closing Phase 1, claiming Phase 1 PASS, or claiming product readiness.
Use at most three separate bounded read-only reviewer agents if genuinely supported; otherwise record BOUNDED_READ_ONLY_REVIEWERS_UNAVAILABLE.

Create docs/reviews/kernel-vertical-slice-builder-review.md as Builder review, not independent acceptance, and cover the required review topics with severities, sources, locations, observations, risks, remediations, validation methods, and dispositions.
Fix all Builder-discovered CRITICAL and HIGH findings within task scope and do not hide MEDIUM or LOW findings.
Update docs/engineering/validation-evidence.md preserving prior evidence and adding KERNEL-VS-001 validation evidence without fabricating command output.
Run final verification commands, run kernel tests at least twice, directly test semantic determinism, and confirm pnpm verify does not mutate tracked diff or create untracked artifacts.

Inspect product source for forbidden imports or usages, classify boundary-validation and rejection-test strings honestly, and ensure product kernel or contracts reason codes do not use the Foundation HARN_ namespace.
Confirm only the four product Verdict values exist and no fifth product Verdict is added.
Confirm package dependency boundaries and no unauthorized production layer exists.
Confirm read-only authority and forbidden paths remain unchanged from the baseline.
Inspect the complete diff before commit.
Commit only on phase1/kernel-vertical-slice-1 with message feat: implement deterministic kernel vertical slice.
Push normally to origin phase1/kernel-vertical-slice-1 without force pushing, without pushing to main, and without merging.
Create a non-draft pull request targeting main with the required title, body content, limitations, Builder review boundary, and known remaining risks.
After push and PR creation, record the PR number, exact remote head SHA, and observed CI status for that exact head without claiming CI success unless observed.

requiredInvariants: Foundation governance JSON status remains VALID.
Kernel tests run at least twice.
Semantic determinism tests directly compare finalized bundle output.
The final pnpm verify run does not alter tracked working diff or create new untracked repository artifacts.
Product source under packages/contracts and packages/kernel has no forbidden production imports or usages except allowed node:crypto deterministic hashing.
No product kernel or contracts reason code uses the HARN_ namespace.
Only ADMISSIBLE, REVISION_REQUIRED, REJECTED, and BLOCKED are product Verdict values.
contracts has no dependency on kernel.
kernel has only the authorized workspace contracts production dependency.
No public registry runtime dependency is added.
Read-only authority and forbidden paths remain unchanged from the baseline.
The complete diff is inspected before commit.
The PR is not merged by the Builder.

requiredArtifacts: commit feat: implement deterministic kernel vertical slice
stopConditions: repository identity mismatch
Phase 1 baseline differs from e2964c726614d67c0e01239463e943b2e21189b2
worktree is not clean before preflight edits
current branch is not main before authorized branch creation
canonical terminology changes are required
Verdict semantic changes are required
Evidence authority class changes are required
Trust semantic changes are required
repository inspection is required
target-code execution is required
verification execution is required
adapter capability modeling or language adapter implementation is required
Python path traversal detection is required
CLI, API, MCP, web, GitHub product integration, SARIF, model provider, or Inference Zone work is required
new external dependencies outside authorized TypeScript and Node types are required
a production runtime dependency from the public package registry is required
a security exception is required
Policy runtime implementation is required
a general product reason-code registry is required
execution boundary implementation is required
a Machine Task Contract would have to be treated as product runtime Trusted Configuration
Builder claim, test names, source comments, repository prose, or model output would have to become Evidence
Authority-Change Preflight would have to be weakened
implementation outside packages/contracts and packages/kernel is required
the PR would need to be merged by the Builder
